seoTitle: How to Connect a GoDaddy Domain to Webflow
metaDescription: Point your GoDaddy domain to Webflow using either the automated Quick Connect wizard or manual DNS record setup.
intro: Getting your GoDaddy domain live on Webflow can be done two ways: an automated one-click connection or manual DNS record entry, and this guide walks through both. You'll learn how to protect your existing email while pointing the domain, and how to confirm SSL and propagation once it's done. It's for anyone launching a Webflow site on a domain purchased through GoDaddy.

steps.heading: Open the custom domain panel in Webflow
steps.body: Go to your project's site settings and find the Custom Domain publishing section.

steps.heading: Choose Quick Connect or manual setup
steps.body: Select the automated OAuth Quick Connect option to authorize GoDaddy directly, or choose manual setup if you prefer to edit DNS records yourself.

steps.heading: Add the required DNS records
steps.body: If going manual, log into GoDaddy's DNS management and add the A record pointing to Webflow's IP and a CNAME record for the www subdomain.

steps.heading: Enter the TXT verification code
steps.body: Copy the one-time verification string from Webflow and paste it as a TXT record in GoDaddy to prove domain ownership.

steps.heading: Confirm propagation and publish
steps.body: Check that your existing MX email records are untouched, run a DNS propagation check, then publish your site to the live domain.

faq.question: Will connecting my domain break my existing email?
faq.answer: It shouldn't, as long as your MX records are left in place during the DNS update, which both the automated and manual methods aim to preserve.

faq.question: Which method is faster, Quick Connect or manual DNS?
faq.answer: Quick Connect is generally faster since it automates the record updates, while manual setup gives you more direct control over each DNS entry.

faq.question: How long does it take for the domain to go live?
faq.answer: DNS propagation can take anywhere from a few minutes to 48 hours depending on your provider and existing record TTLs.

faq.question: Why isn't my SSL certificate showing as active?
faq.answer: SSL provisioning depends on DNS fully propagating first, so give it time and recheck the domain status panel in Webflow.
